vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

verify.rule: All required elements are present in the blueprint.
The mass estimate is within the expected range for the subsystem.
Each P0 internal_feature_target appears as a part_tree child and geometry_ops id or target.
Each interface has a named exact neighboring subsystem label with matching joint family.
Critical joints have visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque/preload intent.
Boundary conditions are defined with fixture points, thermal zones, and pressure faces.

risk.mit: Implement thermal management systems and conduct thermal cycling tests.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance between moving parts leading to jamming.
risk.mit: Review and adjust clearances to ensure proper fit and function.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure under extreme maneuvering loads.
risk.mit: Conduct load tests and structural analysis to validate design.

risk.desc: Risk of improper assembly due to complex interface requirements.
risk.mit: Develop detailed assembly instructions and conduct fit checks.

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate service access for maintenance and repairs.
risk.mit: Ensure service access panels are easily accessible and properly designed.

verify.value: All required fields are present and valid.
verify.rule: Ensure all required fields in the schema are filled out correctly.

verify.value: Mass estimate is provided and within expected range.
verify.rule: Include a mass_est_g value in cad_brief and ensure it is a plausible estimate for the subsystem.

verify.value: All P0 internal_feature_targets are traceable to part_tree children and geometry_ops.
verify.rule: Each P0 feature must have corresponding geometry_ops that reference it by id or target name.

verify.value: All interfaces have reciprocal definitions in adjacent subsystems.
verify.rule: Adjacent interfaces must have matching connection_type, DOF, clearance_mm, and required_feature in neighboring subsystems.

verify.value: Visible locking or preload evidence is present for critical joints.
verify.rule: Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.

verify.value: Boundary conditions are defined with fixture_points, thermal_zones, and pressure_faces.
verify.rule: Include fixture_points, thermal_zones, and pressure_faces for downstream CFD/FEA.

verify.value: Geometry_ops are distributed across the subsystem volume.
verify.rule: Geometry_ops args.at [x,y,z] must be physically distributed across the local subsystem volume.

verify.value: Geometry_ops include a variety of non-box features.
verify.rule: Use drill, pocket, boss, channel, cylinder, chamfer, fillet, pattern, and engrave where appropriate.

verify.value: Motion claims are grounded in a named mechanism type with geometry present.
verify.rule: Design claims motion ('arm rotates', 'finger flexes') but no pivot holes, gear teeth dummies, cam profiles, or linkage bars exist in geometry.

verify.value: Tilt axis, bearing seats, servo/linkage access, and nacelle rotation clearance are visible.
verify.rule: Tilt axis, bearing seats, servo/linkage access, and nacelle rotation clearance must be visible.

verify.value: Wing spar socket, fuselage spine, and payload/battery service paths are separate and inspectable.
verify.rule: Wing/fuselage is a blended block with no interface features.

verify.value: Output remains an educational mockup, avoiding operational flight tuning or control-law details.
verify.rule: Design claims BVLOS capability, flight readiness, or actionable aircraft tuning.

verify.value: Tilt mechanism loads route through bearing seats into wing structure via named fittings, and motor/servo modules remain replaceable through access covers.
verify.rule: Nacelle attaches by an undefined blend; motor and servo are entombed with no removal path or load fitting.

verify.value: A primary datum feature is explicitly named and placed.
verify.rule: No datum reference — assembly alignment is uncontrolled.

verify.value: Adjacent interfaces include fit_class and stack_up_mm.
verify.rule: Interfaces have no tolerance or fit specification.

verify.value: cad_brief includes ordered assembly_sequence with tool, torque, datum per step.
verify.rule: No assembly order — build sequence is ambiguous.

verify.value: Uses seed-characteristic ops: chamfer, pocket, drill, subtract. Subtractive features and finishing features are present.
verify.rule: Only basic box/cylinder ops. Missing subtractive or finishing features typical for this seed.

verify.value: Dimensions match tiltrotor scale and are physically plausible.
verify.rule: Dimensions are placeholder (all 10mm) or unrealistic for the subsystem scale.

risk.mit: Implement a more efficient heat sink design or increase cooling airflow.

risk.desc: Risk of servo linkage misalignment during assembly.
risk.mit: Add alignment guides to the servo linkage and ensure proper assembly sequence.

risk.desc: Possible structural integrity issues if wing spar socket is not properly integrated.
risk.mit: Review and reinforce the wing spar socket design to ensure structural integrity.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance for nacelle rotation.
risk.mit: Review and adjust the clearance arc dimensions to ensure sufficient rotation clearance.

risk.desc: Potential accessibility issues for motor and ESC pockets.
risk.mit: Ensure pockets are designed to allow easy access for maintenance and replacement.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

physics_paths.description: Rotor thrust is transferred through the bearing seats into the wing structure.
Nacelle tilts around the tilt axis, which is supported by the 6309 bearing set.
Heat from the motor is transferred to the heat sink via thermal conduction.
Electrical power is supplied from the ESC to the motor.
Servo drives the worm gear to tilt the nacelle.

internal_feature_targets.description: Axis of rotation for the nacelle tilt mechanism.
Seats for the 6309 bearing set to support the tilt axis.
Mechanical linkage between the servo and the worm gear.
Arc of clearance for the nacelle to rotate freely.
Mounting point for the servo motor.
Pocket to house the electronic speed controller.
Heat sink to dissipate heat from the motor.
Socket to mate with the wing spar for structural integrity.

integration_opportunities.description: Integration of the tilt axis with the nacelle structure.
Integration of the bearing seats with the tilt axis.
Integration of the servo linkage with the tilt mechanism.
Integration of the motor pocket with the nacelle structure.
Integration of the ESC pocket with the nacelle structure.

verification_focus.description: Ensure the tilt axis functions correctly within the specified range.
Ensure the bearing seats are properly integrated and support the tilt axis.
Ensure the servo linkage is properly aligned with the tilt mechanism.
Ensure the motor pocket is accessible for servicing.
Ensure the ESC pocket is accessible for servicing.

generated_parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
generated_parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

requirements: Discipline: integration - role: Nacelle interface datums, harness routing through tilt axis, hatches, connection types and load-path closure
Required evidence_features: interface_datum, harness_channel, service_hatch, tilt_axis, service_window, harness_path
Replaceable module path: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Cable harness visibility: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access not just text: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Harness strain relief and bend radius: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius
Connector accessibility and keying: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling
Datum visibility: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled
Clearance named: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances
Interface count: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums
Standard fit vocabulary: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified
Standard hardware dimensioning: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes
Connection type named: Every inter-part interface names a specific connection type with its DOF and a numeric clearance
Assembly sequence feasible: Parts can be installed in a stated order where every fastener, connector, and mating feature stays reachable
Datum chain continuity: One primary datum part anchors the assembly; each other part locates off a named parent datum
Load path closure: Every part's primary load traces through named interfaces to the base/ground
Structural development direction: Fewer interfaces, parts consolidated where service is preserved, open load paths closed, and redundancy added only at critical joints
Interface minimization: The assembly uses the fewest interfaces that still allow service and manufacture; each remaining interface is justified
P0 feature mapping: P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets
Coordinate distribution: Most geometry_ops include args.at coordinates distributed across the local subsystem volume
Non-box feature variety: Uses drill, pocket, boss, channel, pattern_polar, fillet, chamfer
Per-child geometry density: Every part_tree child has 4+ dedicated geometry_ops
Expected op variety: Uses seed-characteristic ops: chamfer, pocket, drill, subtract
Dimensional realism: Dimensions match tiltrotor scale: 200-1500mm wingspan, 50-300mm fuselage dia
Budget: cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value
Joint evidence: critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent
Training quality: make part_tree children and geometry_ops mutually traceable by id/name
Analysis ready: verify must include a boundary_conditions object with fixture_points, thermal_zones, and pressure_faces

Assembly sequence: cad_brief must include assembly_sequence (ordered list of {step:int, action:install|fasten|align|press|route, target:string, tool:string, torque_Nm_or_force_N:number, datum:string}) and disassembly_sequence for field-serviceable parts
Tolerance stack-up: for each adjacent_interface in the subsystem plan, include stack_up_mm (cumulative tolerance from assembly datum to mating face) and fit_class (e.g. H7/h6, LC2)
Dimensional realism: all dimensions must be physically plausible for the subsystem scale

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to large temperature range (-20 to +50C).
risk.mit: Use thermal expansion materials and design with clearance fits.

risk.desc: Risk of harness strain if bend radius is not respected.
risk.mit: Ensure all harness exits have strain relief geometry and routing respects minimum bend radius.

risk.desc: Service hatch may be difficult to access in confined spaces.
risk.mit: Design hatch with clear access paths and ensure it is reachable without disassembling structure.

risk.desc: Potential for mis-mating connectors if keying is not properly implemented.
risk.mit: Ensure connectors have clear keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating.

risk.desc: Risk of assembly errors if fastener access is not maintained throughout assembly sequence.
risk.mit: Ensure all fasteners remain accessible during assembly and document clear assembly sequence.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

physics_paths.path_description: Tilt-axis harness_channel routes through the tilt axis to connect the nacelle to the wing.
Service hatch allows for the removal of heat from the internal components.
Service window provides access for maintenance and inspection.
Load from the nacelle is transferred through the tilt axis to the wing.
Service window provides access for inspection of the tilt axis.
